proposals.title: Skipped deploy/smoke/security/e2e phases
proposals.rationale: this change modifies SKILL.md instructions and Python utility scripts only, with no deployable services, API endpoints, or UI components.

proposals.title: Per-origin strategy defaults
proposals.rationale: Selected over auto-detection (Approach B) and always-rebase (Approach C) because it leverages existing origin classification with minimal complexity

proposals.title: Expanded spec coverage
proposals.rationale: Added scenarios for all origin groups (agent, dependency, automation), conflict failure, and CLI override. Original spec only covered openspec and dependabot.

proposals.title: Strategy resolution at CLI level
proposals.rationale: `resolve_strategy()` runs in `main()` before calling `merge_pr()`, keeping the core function signature unchanged. This is the least invasive change.

proposals.title: Use args.origin directly
proposals.rationale: Removed unnecessary `getattr()` wrapper since the argument is always defined via `add_argument`

proposals.title: Commit quality enforcement at implementation time
proposals.rationale: Rather than cleaning up history at merge time, require agents to produce clean conventional commits during `/implement-feature`

proposals.title: Explicit parallelizability annotation
proposals.rationale: Added parallelizability section to tasks.md showing 4-wide parallel potential

proposals.title: None sentinel for --strategy
proposals.rationale: Changed default from `"squash"` to `None` so the code can distinguish "operator didn't specify" from "operator chose squash"

proposals.title: Connect origin to discovery output
proposals.rationale: Added note in SKILL.md linking `--origin` to `discover_prs.py` output field

proposals.title: Doc-only approach
proposals.rationale: Minimal delta spec added for merge strategy selection; no heavy formal spec changes

proposals.title: Corrected function references
proposals.rationale: Fixed `merge()` → `merge_pr()` to match actual codebase (line 354)

proposals.title: No repo settings change needed
proposals.rationale: `allow_rebase_merge` was already `true` in the GitHub repo settings. Task 4.1 became a verification-only step.

proposals.title: Fix test docstring grouping
proposals.rationale: Converted bare string literals to comments for clarity

proposals.title: Repo settings update included
proposals.rationale: Enable rebase-merge alongside squash in GitHub repo settings via `gh api`

proposals.title: Universal artifact generation
proposals.rationale: Always generate contracts and work-packages during planning, even for sequential tier. Sequential features get a single `wp-main` package covering full scope.

proposals.title: No-interface features get README stub
proposals.rationale: When a feature has no API, DB, or event interfaces, `contracts/` is created with a `README.md` documenting which sub-types were evaluated and why none apply. User chose this over skipping contracts entirely or requiring at least one contract type.

proposals.title: Parallel implementation of independent SKILL.md changes
proposals.rationale: Dispatched three parallel agents for plan-feature, parallel-review-implementation, and implement-feature SKILL.md edits (no file overlap between them).

proposals.title: Full-fidelity contracts at all tiers
proposals.rationale: No lightweight stubs; same OpenAPI/DB/event schema artifacts regardless of tier.

proposals.title: Replaced "full-fidelity" with precise language
proposals.rationale: Contracts use "the same directory structure and file format as parallel tiers" with only applicable sub-types included.

proposals.title: Phase 2 edits done directly
proposals.rationale: merge-pull-requests SKILL.md and CLAUDE.md changes were small enough to do inline rather than dispatching agents.

proposals.title: Whole-branch review fallback
proposals.rationale: When work-packages are missing (legacy PRs, external contributions), treat entire diff as single review unit rather than skipping or auto-generating packages.

proposals.title: Test tasks rephrased as acceptance criteria
proposals.rationale: Tasks 1.1, 1.3, 1.5 are verification checklists (not unit test code), since this is a SKILL.md change.

proposals.title: Selective staging
proposals.rationale: Only committed our actual changes and their runtime copies, not pre-existing dirty state from the worktree bootstrap.

proposals.title: Always consume contracts in implementation
proposals.rationale: implement-feature reads contracts at all tiers for interface validation, not just parallel+.

proposals.title: Removed spec sync tasks (2.3, 2.4)
proposals.rationale: Delta spec syncing to main specs is handled by `/cleanup-feature` after PR merge, not during implementation.

proposals.title: Added failure/edge-case scenarios
proposals.rationale: Partial contracts, malformed contract files, malformed work-packages.yaml, whole-branch review with contracts but no work-packages.

proposals.title: Extend existing workflow instead of adding a separate factory mode
proposals.rationale: the repository already has the right building blocks in gen-eval, OpenSpec workflow artifacts, validation phases, and feature discovery.

proposals.title: Holdout enforcement uses directory structure + manifest + skill filtering
proposals.rationale: three reinforcing layers without filesystem-level exclusion policies. Directory split makes intent visible; manifest confirms metadata; skill logic enforces at runtime.

proposals.title: Treat public vs holdout as workflow-visible metadata
proposals.rationale: visibility must be enforceable at validation and iteration gates, not just implied by directory names.

proposals.title: Process-analysis generated in /validate-feature only
proposals.rationale: single generation point with all convergence data. Cleanup and merge gates consume read-only.

proposals.title: Add process-analysis as a new optional artifact
proposals.rationale: archive mining needs normalized process outcomes, not only prose and git history.

proposals.title: DTU bootstrap is docs-only; live probes optional
proposals.rationale: keeps bootstrap fast and offline-capable. Low-fidelity DTUs eligible for public scenarios only.

proposals.title: Split task 3.4 into 3.4a/3.4b/3.4c
proposals.rationale: one subtask per consuming skill (iterate-on-implementation, cleanup-feature, merge-pull-requests) for single-commit granularity.

proposals.title: Rescoped task 6.1
proposals.rationale: cross-phase integration smoke test only, not re-running all phase test suites.

proposals.title: Fixed task 1.3 spec refs
proposals.rationale: removed incorrect `software-factory-tooling.3.1-3.2` (External Project Bootstrap), task correctly maps to `gen-eval-framework.4.1-4.3` (Multi-Source Scenario Bootstrap).

proposals.title: Create dedicated roadmap skills
proposals.rationale: Use `plan-roadmap` and `autopilot-roadmap` as new orchestrator skills that reuse current planning and autopilot capabilities.

proposals.title: Added explicit Impact section
proposals.rationale: aligned proposal with affected capability, skills, and docs.

proposals.title: Added machine-readable artifact schemas
proposals.rationale: Created JSON Schema definitions for roadmap.yaml, checkpoint.json, and learning-log entries. These are the primary interface contracts between plan-roadmap and autopilot-roadmap, enabling independent parallel implementation.

proposals.title: Adopt policy-based limit handling
proposals.rationale: Encode time-vs-money trade-offs as explicit execution policies (`wait_if_budget_exceeded`, `switch_if_time_saved`).

proposals.title: Strengthened spec testability
proposals.rationale: added failure/edge scenarios for each requirement area.

proposals.title: Moved shared runtime to dedicated directory
proposals.rationale: Created `skills/roadmap-runtime/` following the established `skills/parallel-infrastructure/` pattern. Avoids duplicating code across consumer skill directories.

proposals.title: Use durable artifact-based context
proposals.rationale: Keep roadmap state, checkpoints, and learning logs on disk to reduce dependence on a single context window.

proposals.title: Improved parallelizability
proposals.rationale: replaced single `wp-main` with decomposed work packages and dependency DAG.

proposals.title: Defined canonical artifact location model
proposals.rationale: Roadmap artifacts live under the roadmap's own OpenSpec change directory. Child changes reference parent via `parent_roadmap` field; bidirectional linking enables traceability.

proposals.title: Clarified assumptions as decisions
proposals.rationale: made policy defaults and state authority explicit.

proposals.title: Redesigned learning-log as progressive disclosure
proposals.rationale: Root `learning-log.md` index with per-item entries in `learnings/` subfolder. Bounds context assembly to O(k) where k = dependency fan-in + recency window. Compaction at 50-entry threshold.

proposals.title: Added cascading vendor failover
proposals.rationale: Policy evaluation is recursive across remaining eligible vendors up to `max_switch_attempts_per_item`, rather than single-hop only.

proposals.title: Added item implementation failure handling
proposals.rationale: Items can transition to `failed` with structured reason; dependents go to `blocked` or `replan_required`. Most common failure mode now has explicit spec coverage.

proposals.title: Added observability and sanitization requirements
proposals.rationale: Structured log events for state transitions, policy decisions, and checkpoint operations. Sanitization contract prevents secret exposure in persisted artifacts.

proposals.title: Regenerated work-packages.yaml with executor fields
proposals.rationale: Added task_type, locks, worktree, verification steps, outputs, scope.deny per established schema pattern.

proposals.title: Added CLAUDE.md update task
proposals.rationale: Task 4.3 for updating the workflow table with roadmap skill entry points. AGENTS.md is a symlink to CLAUDE.md so it updates automatically.

proposals.title: Approach A: In-place migration
proposals.rationale: Two files import fastmcp, no need for adapter layers or parallel modules

proposals.title: Rebase-merge strategy
proposals.rationale: 8 structured commits (plan, dep, server, client, test, docs, review fixes) preserve granular history for git blame/bisect

proposals.title: Pin to >=3.2.3 exclusively
proposals.rationale: Clean break, no dual-version compat shims

proposals.title: All tasks marked complete
proposals.rationale: 2013 tests passing, lint and types clean, all 15 planned tasks were implemented

proposals.title: Migrate SSE to HTTP Streamable
proposals.rationale: SSE is legacy in 3.x, no external consumers depend on it

proposals.title: No open task migration
proposals.rationale: All work completed in this PR; no follow-up needed

proposals.title: Sequential tier
proposals.rationale: Single architectural boundary (agent-coordinator only)

proposals.title: Pure service layer approach
proposals.rationale: Static in-code registry of HelpTopic dataclasses, shared across all three transports. Zero infrastructure dependencies. Selected over auto-generation (quality ceiling too low for workflow guidance) and DB-backed (overkill for 15 topics).

proposals.title: Cherry-pick from dev branch
proposals.rationale: Reused the existing implementation from the dev branch rather than reimplementing from scratch, since all 24 tests and the full service layer were already complete and passing.

proposals.title: Remove repr formatting from error messages
proposals.rationale: All three transports used `f"Unknown topic: {topic!r}"` which embeds Python-specific quoting in API responses. Changed to plain `f"Unknown topic: {topic}"` for cleaner, transport-neutral output.

proposals.title: Static now, extensible later
proposals.rationale: The `get_help_overview` / `get_help_topic` / `list_topic_names` interface is designed so runtime extension (plugin registration) can be added without breaking changes.

proposals.title: Manual conflict resolution for coordination_api.py
proposals.rationale: The cherry-pick had merge conflicts in the health endpoint section. Resolved by keeping the worktree's original inline health endpoint and removing duplicate `/live`, `/ready`, `/health` endpoints that referenced a non-existent `_database_health()` helper.

proposals.title: Add hint field to CLI JSON error response
proposals.rationale: The MCP and HTTP transports included a `hint` field in unknown-topic errors, but CLI JSON mode omitted it. Added for cross-transport schema consistency per spec scenario 9.

proposals.title: Transport-agnostic content
proposals.rationale: Same help content regardless of caller transport (MCP, HTTP, CLI). Avoids content branching complexity.

proposals.title: Runtime import pattern for TestClient
proposals.rationale: Used `TYPE_CHECKING` guard for type hints with runtime import inside the fixture to avoid import-time dependency on FastAPI in the test module header.

proposals.title: Show all topics always
proposals.rationale: No capability-aware filtering. Agents see all 15 topics regardless of which services are online, enabling discovery of capabilities they might want enabled.

proposals.title: No auth on help endpoints
proposals.rationale: Help is a discovery mechanism; requiring auth would defeat the purpose for agents that haven't authenticated yet.

proposals.title: Named Tunnel approach over DNS Proxy
proposals.rationale: Provides full service mesh (API + MCP SSE + OpenBao), eliminates Railway hosting cost, and creates provider-portable stable URLs

proposals.title: Added wildcard subdomain support to SSRF allowlist
proposals.rationale: The existing _validate_url() did exact hostname matching only. Spec required wildcard support. Added ~10 lines to parse wildcard entries and match subdomains.

proposals.title: Full service mesh DNS
proposals.rationale: Subdomains for all services (coord/mcp/vault) rather than just the API, enabling cloud agents to use all transport options

proposals.title: Cloudflare profile extends Railway (not base)
proposals.rationale: Inherits Railway's Postgres DSN, API host/port, workers. Only overrides coordination_allowed_hosts with wildcard domain.

proposals.title: Both Docker Compose and standalone service hosting
proposals.rationale: Docker for development workflow, systemd/launchd for always-on production

proposals.title: No nested profile interpolation
proposals.rationale: The profile loader regex does not support nested variable references. Used simple wildcard pattern instead, with COORDINATION_ALLOWED_HOSTS env var overriding at runtime.

proposals.title: Sequential tier
proposals.rationale: Feature is focused on single architectural boundary (networking/deployment infrastructure)

proposals.title: Comprehensive runbook
proposals.rationale: Combined all documentation tasks (zone setup, Railway custom domain, tunnel, agent config, secrets) into a single docs/cloudflare-setup.md with clear sections for each path.

proposals.title: Coordinator-only (no offline fallback)
proposals.rationale: Offline issue tracking is a subset of what OpenSpec tasks.md already provides. The coordinator is always running during meaningful work.

proposals.title: Full beads replacement
proposals.rationale: Remove all bd CLI references, hooks, skills, and .beads/ directory.

proposals.title: Extend work_queue (Approach A)
proposals.rationale: Add labels, parent_id, issue_type, assignee, comments to existing table rather than creating a separate issues table.

proposals.title: IssueService as separate class (D1)
proposals.rationale: Delegates to DB directly, not through WorkQueueService, to avoid inheriting agent-coordination semantics (policy checks, guardrails) inappropriate for issue CRUD.

proposals.title: task_type='issue' discrimination (D5)
proposals.rationale: Issues set task_type='issue' so get_work won't accidentally claim them.

proposals.title: Full three-phase scope
proposals.rationale: Deliver all phases (model hints, archetype registry, coordinator routing) rather than Phase 1 only. Rationale: centralized archetypes avoid config duplication and enable complexity-based escalation.

proposals.title: Extend existing fallback chains
proposals.rationale: Archetypes override primary model but reuse agents.yaml fallback sequences. Avoids duplicating fallback config per archetype.

proposals.title: System prompts per archetype
proposals.rationale: Each archetype carries a role-specific system prompt prefix composed with task prompts. Improves output quality by framing agent behavior per role.

proposals.title: Complexity-based escalation
proposals.rationale: Implementer archetype auto-escalates sonnet to opus when work-package exceeds thresholds (>3 write dirs, >2 deps, >500 LOC, or explicit complexity flag).

proposals.title: Layered Archetype System (Approach A)
proposals.rationale: Selected over skill-embedded mapping (B) and work-package driven (C) for centralized config and coordinator routing.

proposals.title: Rebase on main after 19 commits
proposals.rationale: Cherry-picked original plan commit onto current main (5089f60) to incorporate speculative merge trains, autopilot rename, and other recent changes.

proposals.title: Remove `fallback_strategy` from spec
proposals.rationale: Field was listed in Requirement 1 but undefined elsewhere. Fallback behavior is fully covered by design decision D4 (extend existing fallback chains).

proposals.title: Add failure scenario for fallback chain exhaustion
proposals.rationale: Requirement 5 only had a 429-fallback success scenario. Added scenario for when all models in the chain are exhausted.

proposals.title: Fix escalation dependency threshold
proposals.rationale: Scenario had 2 deps but said more-than-2. Changed to 3 deps to match threshold consistently.

proposals.title: Document merge train coexistence (D7)
proposals.rationale: Added design decision D7 establishing that archetype routing and merge train routing are orthogonal filtering dimensions in `claim()`. Archetype filters by agent capability; merge trains filter by partition/wave ordering.

proposals.title: Add missing fallback chain test task (3.4.0)
proposals.rationale: Requirement 5 had no corresponding test task. Added with `respx` mock approach for 429 simulation.

proposals.title: Add failure scenario for invalid archetype in work package
proposals.rationale: Requirement 7 only had success scenarios. Added validation failure scenario for invalid archetype name pattern.

proposals.title: Add missing archetypes.yaml-not-found scenario
proposals.rationale: D5 graceful degradation now has explicit spec scenario.

proposals.title: Keep all 3 phases in scope
proposals.rationale: Confirmed full scope (model hints → archetype registry → coordinator routing) rather than scoping down to Phase 1 only.

proposals.title: Fix `wp-skill-archetype-refs` dependency
proposals.rationale: Added `wp-skill-model-hints` to depends_on to prevent parallel file conflicts on shared SKILL.md files.

proposals.title: Correct contracts README
proposals.rationale: Acknowledged Phase 3 API parameter extensions as additive contract changes.

proposals.title: Elevate from Draft to Approved
proposals.rationale: Status changed after validating all artifacts pass OpenSpec validation, work-packages schema/DAG/locks, and parallel zones checks.

proposals.title: Clarify prompt composition ownership
proposals.rationale: Expanded task 2.2.2 to include `compose_prompt()` function, resolving the ownership gap for design decision D2.

proposals.title: Clarify resolve_model() callsite
proposals.rationale: Skills call resolve_model() at dispatch time; Agent tool receives concrete model.

proposals.title: Clarify escalation metadata flow
proposals.rationale: Updated task 2.2.3 to specify that dispatching skills pass package metadata as a dict; `resolve_model()` does not load YAML itself.

proposals.title: Move API lock keys
proposals.rationale: From wp-queue-routing to wp-api-mcp-integration where coordination_api.py is actually modified.

proposals.title: Measurable scenario criteria
proposals.rationale: Replaced subjective "focused on cross-cutting concerns" and "report results concisely" with pattern-matchable phrases.

proposals.title: Full Train in Coordinator
proposals.rationale: Speculative merge train logic extends merge_queue.py rather than being a standalone skill. Rationale: single source of truth for merge ordering, reuses existing feature registry resource claims, ACID transactional guarantees for train state transitions.

proposals.title: D8: Post-speculation claim validation
proposals.rationale: After speculative ref creation, validate actual file changes match declared resource claims. Prevents unsafe partition assignment from dishonest or incorrect agent claims.

proposals.title: Requirement ID index (R1-R13)
proposals.rationale: Added explicit requirement IDs and cross-reference index to agent-coordinator spec. Prevents task/spec mismatch as plan evolves.

proposals.title: Refresh-architecture RPC contract (HIGH)
proposals.rationale: Created `contracts/internal/refresh-architecture-rpc.yaml` defining `is_graph_stale`, `trigger_refresh`, and `get_refresh_status`. Previously task 5.8 had an implicit cross-service orchestration dependency with no defined contract. Server side lives in wp-build-graph (new task 3.8, new file `skills/refresh-architecture/scripts/rpc_server.py`), client side lives in wp-integration (new task 5.8a, new file `agent-coordinator/src/refresh_rpc_client.py`).

proposals.title: File-path-to-namespace mapping function (Finding #1)
proposals.rationale: Added tasks 1.4a/1.4b to wp-contracts defining `file_path_to_namespaces(path) -> set[str]` in merge_train_types.py. Mapping is declared as a list of `(glob_pattern, namespace)` tuples at module level. Rules reference the 9 namespaces in `docs/lock-key-namespaces.md`. Heuristic: unmapped paths are NOT treated as mismatches — they are out-of-scope for logical-namespace checking (file-path locks still apply). Task 2.6 (claim validation impl) now depends on 1.4b and specifies the exact algorithm using `actual_namespaces - declared_namespaces`.

proposals.title: Sentinel return type for RPC failures, not exceptions
proposals.rationale: RefreshRpcClient and compute_affected_tests return RefreshClientUnavailable or None rather than raising on transport errors. The load-bearing invariant that merge-train progress must never be blocked on refresh-architecture is encoded in the type signature, so mypy enforces handling at every call site. A forgotten except clause would silently freeze the train; a forgotten sentinel check is a type error.

proposals.title: Full Stacked Diffs
proposals.rationale: Work packages land directly on main behind feature flags, not collected on feature branches. Rationale: maximum merge train throughput by reducing merge unit from feature to package; requires feature flag infrastructure.

proposals.title: D9: BLOCKED entry recovery lifecycle
proposals.rationale: BLOCKED entries can be manually re-enqueued or auto-re-evaluated after 1 hour. Prevents accumulation of permanently stuck entries.

proposals.title: R12: Automatic Flag Creation
proposals.rationale: Added spec requirement for auto-creating feature flags when first stacked-diff package is enqueued. Closes the gap between proposal Feature 4 and the spec.

proposals.title: git merge-tree conflict detection method
proposals.rationale: Task 2.4 now specifies dual detection (exit code AND stderr parsing), required git 2.38+ version check on adapter startup, and explicit `MergeTreeResult` return shape. Previously the task referenced `git merge-tree` without specifying how to detect conflicts.

proposals.title: R12 scope conflict resolution (Finding #2)
proposals.rationale: Moved the enqueue-flag-integration out of wp-feature-flags into wp-train-engine as new tasks 2.15 (tests) and 2.16 (implementation). wp-feature-flags now only owns the flag module itself and exposes `create_flag()` as a public function (task 4.4). wp-train-engine now depends on BOTH wp-contracts AND wp-feature-flags. wp-feature-flags priority lowered from 3 → 2 (it's now a sibling dependency of wp-train-engine, not a successor). This keeps merge_queue.py ownership clean (only wp-train-engine writes to it).

proposals.title: Hexagonal split: pure engine vs DB-backed service
proposals.rationale: The engine (merge_train.py) operates on in-memory TrainEntry lists with zero DB knowledge (186+ pure unit tests, no DB setup). The service (merge_train_service.py) wraps it with load/save methods, authorization, and refresh-architecture probing. This split let wp-integration layer HTTP/MCP/sweeper surfaces on the service without touching the engine.

proposals.title: Priority Eject Recovery
proposals.rationale: Failed train entries are ejected and re-queued at lower priority; independent successors continue without re-speculation. Rationale: fastest recovery, avoids O(N) re-speculation cost of full bisect model.

proposals.title: D10: Affected-test traversal bounds
proposals.rationale: BFS capped at 10K nodes with fallback to full test suite. Ensures predictable query latency on large graphs.

proposals.title: Scope boundaries documented
proposals.rationale: Added explicit "In scope (Phase 1)" vs "Deferred (Phase 2)" section to proposal. DAG scheduling integration explicitly deferred.

proposals.title: Pipeline refactoring scoped for task 3.7
proposals.rationale: Task 3.7 now itemizes the 5 sub-steps required to insert `test_linker` into `compile_architecture_graph.py`. The existing pipeline splits at stage 3 (sequential graph mutations) into stages 4-6 (concurrent read-only), so test_linker must go in the sequential phase as stage 3b, NOT as a drop-in insert. Estimated ~50 LOC.

proposals.title: D12: Max-eject threshold + ABANDONED state (Finding #3)
proposals.rationale: Added new design decision D12 bounding repeat-ejection via `MAX_EJECT_COUNT` (default 3) in merge_train_types.py. New terminal state ABANDONED added to MergeStatus enum. Added new spec requirement R14 (Max-Eject Threshold) with 3 scenarios: below threshold, reaches threshold, manual re-enqueue reset. Task 2.8 (eject impl) expanded with ABANDONED transition logic. Manual re-enqueue resets `eject_count = 0` and restores original `merge_priority`. TrainEntry metadata now tracks `eject_count` and `last_eject_reason`.

proposals.title: Subprocess runner Protocol for testability
proposals.rationale: Both RefreshRpcClient and compute_affected_tests accept an injectable Runner callable matching the signature of subprocess.run. Tests use a _FakeRunner that queues exact responses. This avoids the global monkeypatch footgun where fakes leak across tests.

proposals.title: Incremental Build Graph
proposals.rationale: Test analysis ships in three phases: import-level (P1) → transitive closure (P2) → fixture-aware (P3). Rationale: ship value early, iterate based on false-positive/negative rates.

proposals.title: D11: Train operation authorization
proposals.rationale: compose_train requires trust level 3+, eject requires ownership or level 3+. Prevents unauthorized train manipulation.

proposals.title: Impact section added
proposals.rationale: Proposal now has quantified impact claims mapped to success criteria.

proposals.title: Cross-partition merge ordering pseudo-code
proposals.rationale: Added wave-based merge algorithm to design.md D4 and tightened task 2.12 to reference it. The algorithm: build ready-graph → topo-sort → advance in waves where each wave parallel-merges all currently-ready nodes → rebase partition speculative refs after cross-partition merges. Raises `TrainDeadlockError` if a wave is empty while pending remains.

proposals.title: Git adapter contract alignment (Finding #4)
proposals.rationale: Updated `contracts/internal/git-adapter-api.yaml` `create_speculative_ref` output to include both `tree_oid` (for caching) and `commit_sha` (new commit SHA after ref materialization), plus `success`, `conflict_files`, `error`. Removed the ambiguous `ref_sha` field. Task 2.4 now uses matching field names and documents the three distinct failure modes (conflict, non-conflict error, success). This eliminates the MergeTreeResult naming mismatch.

proposals.title: GIN + BTREE expression indexes for JSONB sub-doc queries
proposals.rationale: Migration 020 pairs a GIN index on the whole metadata merge_queue sub-doc (for containment and ad-hoc queries) with BTREE expression indexes on the hot-path equality predicates (train_id, partition_id, and train_position cast to int). The int cast is non-optional — lexicographic ordering silently corrupts numeric sorts.

proposals.title: Lightweight Feature Flags
proposals.rationale: Environment variable override with flags.yaml fallback. Rationale: minimal infrastructure, sufficient for gating incomplete features. No runtime flag service needed initially.

proposals.title: Scope separation: merge_train_types.py
proposals.rationale: Extracted shared data types from merge_queue.py into merge_train_types.py to resolve write-scope overlap between wp-contracts and wp-train-engine.

proposals.title: Missing failure/edge scenarios
proposals.rationale: Added 8 new scenarios across both specs: invalid ref names, empty test files, non-standard test locations, missing modules, relative imports, merge conflicts during speculation, invalid decomposition values, state transition triggers.

proposals.title: Feature flag missing-file fallback (LOW)
proposals.rationale: Explicit failure-mode table added to design.md D7 and task 4.2: missing flags.yaml → empty registry (not crash), malformed flags.yaml → FlagsConfigError at startup (fail loud), orphaned flag reference → returns False (safe removal), undeclared FF_* env var → ignored with warning (fail closed).

proposals.title: Periodic compose_train sweep (Finding #5)
proposals.rationale: Added new task 5.9 in wp-integration implementing `MERGE_TRAIN_SWEEP_INTERVAL_SECONDS` (default 60s) background task via `asyncio.create_task`. Added new spec scenario under R1 covering periodic invocation (catches exceptions, cancellable on shutdown, no-op on empty queue, testable single-pass mode). Updated design.md Data Flow to document both triggers: on-enqueue AND periodic sweep.

proposals.title: Background sweeper swallows every exception by design
proposals.rationale: MergeTrainSweeper.run_once wraps compose_train in a bare except Exception because a crashed sweeper freezes the train indefinitely, which is worse than missing a cycle. Enforced by test_run_once_swallows_exceptions, which asserts a RuntimeError inside compose_train does not propagate.

proposals.title: Fail-closed feature flags
proposals.rationale: Reject any FF_* env var not declared in flags.yaml, preventing injection of undeclared flags.

proposals.title: affected_tests signature alignment (Finding #6)
proposals.rationale: Updated spec codebase-analysis to match the contract signature `affected_tests(changed_files: list[str], graph_path: str | None = None) -> list[str] | None`. Added scenario documenting the `graph_path` parameter as a test-only / out-of-tree override. The spec's "stale graph" scenario already described the `None` return; this iteration fixed the signature mismatch.

proposals.title: Stateful integration fakes, not MagicMock, for full-lifecycle tests
proposals.rationale: _StatefulRegistry and _StatefulDB round-trip DB updates back through metadata merge_queue so a compose-persist-reload cycle observes the new state. Critical for the BLOCKED recovery scenario: a MagicMock returning a stale list would have silently passed a broken implementation.

proposals.title: Git adapter injection prevention
proposals.rationale: Ref name regex validation + shell=False exclusively. Prevents command injection via branch names.

proposals.title: Flag archival deferred (Finding #7)
proposals.rationale: Moved flag archival mechanism to Phase 2 Scope Boundaries in proposal.md. "Release cycle" was undefined operationally, and defining it would require deciding between git-tag, time-window, or manual-trigger semantics — out of scope. Phase 1 supports `disabled`/`enabled` only. Task 4.3 updated to reflect the new scope.

proposals.title: HTTP vs MCP authorization error shape divergence
proposals.rationale: HTTP endpoints raise HTTPException 403 on TrainAuthorizationError; MCP tools return a dict with success false and reason authorization_denied. Different transports, different conventions — each caller gets the shape its stack expects.

proposals.title: agent_id resolved from principal, never from the request body
proposals.rationale: The eject endpoint reads agent_id from resolve_identity on the X-API-Key header, not a body field. This closes the spoofing vector where a legitimate caller could eject any other agent entry by lying in the payload.

proposals.title: Effects-before-write ordering for crash safety
proposals.rationale: merge_queue.enqueue for stacked-diff features creates the feature flag before the DB update. A post-flag crash leaves a harmless orphan flag (reused on retry); a post-DB crash would leave an unguarded stacked-diff in the queue. Rule: effects whose absence corrupts state go before the write.
